description: Test cases verifying the skill correctly derives test intents + pass/fail criteria FROM SOURCE CODE (offline, no app run). Two layers: (1) static-structure cases that check frontmatter / allowed-tools / no SPMExample hardcode in body / vocabulary compliance / 5-step methodology coverage, all without reading any real source; (2) method cases that drive the 5-step workflow against a placeholder app <MyApp> (fictional LoginService / LoginViewModel / LoginVC, no real class names or line numbers) and check the skill's output shape, vocabulary compliance, and source-grounding. The only SPMExample reference appears in the examples pointer at the end of the body (docs/skills/examples/spmexample-login/), never inside generic examples.

evals.prompt: Check the static structure of the ios-test-intent SKILL.md (offline, no app run, no source read): frontmatter must have name / description / allowed-tools in that exact order; allowed-tools must list only offline source-analysis tools (Read / Glob / Grep) and NO mcp__iOSDriver__* tools (this skill never operates the app); the body must NOT hardcode SPMExample values; the body must cover the 5-step methodology (Step 0 scope → Step 1 Service → Step 2 ViewModel → Step 3 ViewController → Step 4 map to criteria → Step 5 cross-flow side effects).
evals.expected_output:
Should verify offline (no app run, no source read):
1. frontmatter has all three fields present in the correct order — grep -E '^(name|description|allowed-tools):' on SKILL.md returns exactly 3 matches
2. allowed-tools lists ONLY: Read, Glob, Grep. Must NOT contain any mcp__iOSDriver__* entry (this is an offline-analysis skill; iOSDriver tools belong to ios-test-runner / ios-ui-*). Optional codegraph_explore is allowed only if explicitly justified; the core set is Read/Glob/Grep
3. name field value matches directory name (ios-test-intent)
4. description is Chinese + English keyword mix containing at minimum: 'test intent', 'pass criteria', 'fail criteria', '测试意图', '读源代码' (per skill-template.md description rule)
5. Body covers the 5-step methodology — grep -c 'Step 0' returns >= 1, 'Step 1' >= 1, 'Step 2' >= 1, 'Step 3' >= 1, 'Step 4' >= 1, 'Step 5' >= 1
6. Body covers the allowed vocabulary table — grep -c 'textExists' >= 1, 'targetExists' >= 1, 'targetGone' >= 1, 'alert' >= 1
7. Body states the forbidden tokens (path / accessibilityIdentifier / viewSnapshotID must NOT appear inside criterion values) — grep -c 'accessibilityIdentifier' >= 1 (the prohibition itself is documented)
8. No SPMExample hardcoding — grep -nE 'com\.coo\.SPMExample|065CC8DB|00008030' on SKILL.md returns empty (OK); the only allowed spmexample mention is the examples pointer near the end (docs/skills/examples/spmexample-login/)
9. No real test credentials as step content — grep -nE 'test.*123456|123456.*test' on SKILL.md returns empty (OK); only <seed-user> / <seed-password> placeholders are allowed in examples
10. Six required sections (目标 / 何时使用 / 工作原理 / 关键产物 / 常见错误与判别 / 相关 skill) all present and in order per skill-template.md
11. Generic examples use placeholder app — grep -c '<MyApp>' >= 1, grep -c '<LoginService>' >= 1; real SPMExample class names (AuthService, LoginViewController) do NOT appear in generic examples

evals.prompt: Use the offline 5-step workflow to derive ONE 'login success' test intent for a placeholder app <MyApp>. The user provides a description of the login code: <LoginViewModel>.<login> guards username/password non-empty (immediate); then calls <LoginService>.<login> which looks up the user in an in-memory store, checks password, has a networkDelay (~1.5s); on success <LoginVC>.<loginButtonTapped> calls <navigateToHome> which does setViewControllers([homeVC]) + hidesBackButton=true. Seed account <seed-user>/<seed-password> is preloaded in <LoginService>.init. Home screen shows text "<欢迎语>".
evals.expected_output:
Should produce ONE intent object WITHOUT reading any real SPMExample source (the user provided the description) and WITHOUT calling any iOSDriver tool:
1. id = 'login-success', flow = 'login'
2. pass_criteria containing textExists with the home-screen welcome text placeholder AND targetGone('导航返回按钮') for the hidesBackButton behavior
3. fail_criteria containing alert (success path should have none) AND textContains with a placeholder failure-message text (NOT the literal SPMExample '用户名或密码错误' — the user did not provide that string)
4. timing = '约 N 秒后' or '约 1.5s 后' derived from networkDelay (the skill reads the delay from the user's description, not a guess)
5. NO path / accessibilityIdentifier / viewSnapshotID in any criterion value
6. business_points reference <LoginViewModel>.<login>, <LoginService>.<login>, <LoginVC>.<loginButtonTapped>/<navigateToHome> (placeholder symbols, NOT real class names like AuthService / LoginViewController)
7. source_refs use placeholder file:symbol form (e.g. '<LoginService>.swift:<login 方法>') — NOT real line numbers
8. depends_on.seed_data mentions <seed-user>/<seed-password> (NOT test/123456)
9. The skill must NOT fabricate elements not in the user's description (e.g., no '记住我 复选框' criterion)

evals.prompt: Analyze a placeholder app <MyApp> login code where <LoginService>.<login> throws the SAME error type for 'user not found' AND 'wrong password', both mapping to the SAME user-facing message (a deliberate security-blurring design). User describes: the two branches are intentionally indistinguishable to the user. Produce two intents and decide whether to fabricate distinct messages.
evals.expected_output:
Should correctly identify the indistinguishability WITHOUT reading any real source (description provided):
1. Produce TWO intents: login-user-not-found and login-wrong-password
2. Both intents' fail_criteria use the SAME textContains value (the shared error message) — because the source maps both branches to one message
3. Set cross_flow_verifiable noting the two are deliberately indistinguishable (security blurring), so neither intent can be told apart by UI text alone — execution layer must differentiate by seed-data setup (register the user first for the 'user not found' branch to become 'wrong password')
4. Must NOT fabricate distinct error messages (e.g., must not invent '用户不存在' vs '密码错误') — fabrication violates the source-grounding rule (Step 1 'error → 文案映射' + Best Practice B 'hallucinated elements')
5. Each criterion carries description + timing; timing on these failure branches = '约 N 秒后(服务端)' since they go through <LoginService>
6. known_defect should NOT be set here — the blurring is a deliberate security design, not a semantic defect (unlike the resetPassword case where the message is semantically WRONG for the flow)

evals.prompt: For placeholder app <MyApp>, determine whether the login failure branch clears the password field, and produce the appropriate test intent. The user provides the VC source description: <LoginVC>.<loginButtonTapped> failure branch only calls showError() and does NOT clear passwordTextField.text. There is NO 'iOS standard behavior' assumption allowed — read what the VC actually does.
evals.expected_output:
Should correctly respect source over generic iOS assumptions (the core Best Practice C trap):
1. Recognize from the user's VC description that the password field is RETAINED on failure (not cleared)
2. Must NOT assume the generic 'iOS standard clears password on failure' — that assumption is explicitly forbidden in Step 3 and Best Practice C
3. Produce a 'login-password-retained-on-failure' intent whose criteria verify retention INDIRECTLY: since secure text fields do not expose their text via ui_inspect, verify by submitting again WITHOUT re-entering the password — if retained, the second submit reaches <LoginService> after ~N seconds and shows the shared failure message; if cleared, the second submit fails immediately with '请输入密码' (the ViewModel empty-check guard fires)
4. fail_criteria for the 'cleared' case would be textExists('请输入密码') with timing='立即(客户端校验)' (this is how to tell the two behaviors apart at runtime)
5. source_refs cite <LoginVC>.swift:<loginButtonTapped> (placeholder symbol form, NOT a real line number like 230-232)
6. The skill should explain in the intent description that secure-text-field text cannot be read directly, justifying the indirect verification — this is a methodology point, not a workaround

evals.prompt: For placeholder app <MyApp>, derive a test intent for the reset-password flow where <LoginService>.<resetPassword> failure (user not found OR email mismatch) maps to the message '用户名或密码错误'. This message is semantically WRONG for a reset flow (should describe '用户不存在或邮箱不匹配'). Decide how to handle the semantic defect in the test intent.
evals.expected_output:
Should correctly distinguish 'business rule to test as-is' from 'implementation defect to flag':
1. Identify the semantic defect: reset-password failure uses a login-style message that does not describe the actual failure (user-not-found / email-mismatch)
2. Set known_defect=true on the intent so human review is flagged
3. pass_criteria / fail_criteria MUST STILL assert the ACTUAL current text '用户名或密码错误' — NOT the 'correct' text '用户不存在或邮箱不匹配'. Otherwise the test would be non-deterministic (failing on real behavior)
4. The description field on each criterion must flag the semantic defect for human review, e.g. '当前实际文案为 X,疑似缺陷(语义与重置流程不符),人工裁决;判据仍以实际行为为准保证确定性'
5. Must NOT silently 'fix' the message in the criterion (that would test an imagined behavior, violating source-grounding)
6. Must NOT drop the criterion (that would leave the failure branch untested)
7. This contrasts with eval case 3 (security blurring) — there the shared message is deliberate design (no known_defect); here the message is genuinely wrong for the flow (known_defect=true). The skill must articulate the difference
